project.title: Identification of the FTO O-GlcNAcylation sites by LC-MS/MS
project.projectDescription: We determined the O-GlcNAcylation sites of FTO by LC-MS/MS.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Immunoprecipitated full-length or N-terminal fragment FTO from RAW264.7 cells was subjected to SDS‒PAGE. The corresponding gel bands containing full-length or N-terminal FTO were stained with Coomassie blue, and then the corresponding bands were excised, for LC‒MS/MS analysis (Shanghai Yisuan Biotechnology Technology Co., Ltd Shanghai, China). The peptides were analyzed by Orbitrap fusion lumos coupled to an EASY-nanoLC 1200 system (Thermo Fisher Scientific, MA, USA).
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Peak lists were analyzed using Sequest against a murine UniProt database. Tandem mass spectra were processed by PEAKS Studio version 10.6 (Bioinformatics Solutions Inc., Waterloo,Canada). PEAKS DB was set up to search the database of target assuming trypsin as the digestion enzyme. PEAKS DB were searched with a fragment ion mass tolerance of 0.02 Da and a parent ion tolerance of 7 ppm. Carbamidomethylation (C) was specified as the fixed modification. Oxidation (M), Deamidation (NQ), Acetylation (Protein N-term), N-acetylhexosamine (HexNAc, +203.0794 Da) residues were specified as the variable modifications. The peptides with -10lgP≥20 and the proteins with -10lgP≥20 and containing at least 1unique peptide were filtered.

labPIs.title: Dr
labPIs.affiliation: Wuhan University School of Basic Medical Sciences
labPIs.name: Xiao-Lian Zhang

references.referenceLine: Zhang L, Liu M, Xie Y, Yuan BF, Peng Z, Xiong J, Zhang XL. FTO O-GlcNAcylation promotes TRIM21-mediated FTO ubiquitination degradation to sustain the negative feedback control of macrophage inflammation. Front Immunol. 2025 16:1593243

Characteristics[Bait]: Flag-tagged full-length or N-terminal fragment FTO
Characteristics[CleavageAgent]: assuming trypsin as the digestion enzyme
Characteristics[AlkylationReagent]: Iodoacetamide of Cys residues was selected as a fixed modification.
Characteristics[SpikedCompound]: incubation with 50 μM biotin-DBCO (Click Chemistry Tool, USA)
Comment[Instrument]: Orbitrap fusion lumos coupled to an EASY-nanoLC 1200 system (Thermo Fisher Scientific, MA, USA)
Comment[PrecursorMassTolerance]: a parent ion tolerance of 7 ppm
Comment[FragmentMassTolerance]: fragment ion mass tolerance of 0.02 Da
Comment[EnrichmentMethod]: immunoprecipitated with anti-FTO and protein-A/G magnetic beads
incubated with the anti-Flag antibody plus protein-A/G magnetic beads
Comment[GradientTime]: The LC run started with a gradient from 2% to 15% acetonitrile for 30 min, ramping linearly to 50% for 42 min, and followed by a gradient to 100% acetonitrile within 1 min, which was maintained for 2 min.
